taxonomy: Pappobolus argenteus is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and widespread group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This family is further divided into various subfamilies, and Pappobolus argenteus belongs to the subfamily Heliantoideae. The genus Pappobolus is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas, and it is closely related to other genera in the same family. The classification of Pappobolus argenteus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants, highlighting its unique characteristics and adaptations. Further research may be needed to determine the exact classification and relationships within the Pappobolus genus.

intro: Pappobolus argenteus, commonly known as the silver Pappobolus, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
